summary: Full E2E testing of Maré Alta - Brazilian fishing weather forecast landing page. All 12 backend pytest tests pass (health, presets, geocode Santos/Manaus, weather with/without marine, favorites CRUD). All frontend flows tested and passing: landing/hero, presets dropdown (river preset shows 'Sem dados marítimos'), search (Ubatuba returned 7 results, click loads dashboard), favorites toggle + counter + localStorage persistence after reload, Windy iframe rendered, refresh button, hourly (12 cards) + daily (7 cards) + wind (km/h) + pressure (hPa) + solunar + tides (4 alternating events) all show real data.

minor.issue: marine=false returns marine_available=None (null) rather than false; frontend correctly treats null as falsy, so no functional impact

issues: Search input placeholder text is legible, contrast good. Overall pt-BR translations and light theme render correctly.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is defined AFTER first usage (line 179 uses logger, defined at 308). Currently no crash because geocode never errors on happy path, but a failure before logger definition would raise NameError. Move logging.basicConfig + logger = getLogger up to top of file.

server.py estimate_tides: returns up to 9 events across range k=-4..4 filtered to 0<=t<24; typically produces 3-5 events per day depending on transit time. Docstring/spec says '4 alternating' - actual count varies. Not a bug, but the fixed spec should be documented as 'up to 5'.
server.py estimate_tides tide type alternation logic uses `k % 2`; when k is negative in Python, k%2 for k=-3 returns 1, so alternation stays consistent - verified correct.
server.py surface_pressure trend: works well but relies on hourly[i-3]; if user loads near start of day (i<3), diff compares against same-day early hours (fine, no crash).
server.py CORS middleware is added AFTER app.include_router - order is fine in FastAPI but conventional to add before.

App.js: category comparisons for marine: `loc.category !== 'rio' && loc.category !== 'barragem'` - correct for the 4 preset categories (praia/mar-aberto/rio/barragem). Search results category='busca' will trigger marine=true even for inland cities; consider heuristic or defer marine call.
No pagination/limit on GET /api/favorites (200 hardcoded) - fine for a personal-use app but noted.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_fav_santos_zzz favorite. Cleaned Ubatuba favorite added during frontend testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 12 backend pytest tests in /app/backend/tests/test_mare_alta_backend.py pass against public URL. Frontend fully functional. When testing river presets, wait ~5s after click - initial fetch race with previous state can briefly show stale marine data. Search results have category='busca' -> triggers marine=true even for inland cities (backend gracefully returns marine_available=null if API has no data for the point). Windy iframe is third-party embed, verified only by src attribute.
rca of the issue: No functional issue. First river-preset test flake resolved by increasing wait to 5-6s after selecting preset for the API call + React state update to complete.
